sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 2 actions in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: UP, RIGHT.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 44, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Knowledge ledger carried from earlier turns:
Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: {'RIGHT': 'Moves active green frame right by 3 cols VERIFIED step 8', 'LEFT': 'Moves active green frame left by 3 cols VERIFIED step 2', 'UP': 'Moves active green frame up by 3 rows VERIFIED step 4', 'DOWN': 'Moves active green frame down by 3 rows VERIFIED step 6', 'MOUSE': 'Clicking on green frame selects it (gives white center) VERIFIED step 7'}
- Object taxonomy: {'g': 'gray background', 'w': 'white panels/squares', 'p': 'purple vertical bar (barrier between panels)', 'N': 'light green 3x3 frames (movable tokens)', 'c': 'charcoal 5x5 frames (targets)', 'W': 'white single pixel (selected token center)', 'B': 'black single pixel (unselected token center)'}
- HUD map: {'bottom_bar': 'row 63, charcoal strip'}
- Win pattern: {'rule': 'Move each green frame into a charcoal target frame. Select frame with MOUSE, then use arrow keys to move it.'}
Current level:
- World model: Left frame in left target (rows 33-35, cols 12-14). Right frame selected (white center) at rows 30-32, cols 27-29. Purple barrier at cols 33-38, rows 21-41 (continuous, no gaps). Right target at rows 26-30, cols 44-48 (behind barrier). Frame moves 3 cells per arrow key press. Frame can only move on white panel areas.
- Goal model: Move right frame to right target (rows 26-30, cols 44-48). Barrier blocks direct RIGHT movement. Need to find alternate path or mechanic.
- Recent findings: ['Left frame successfully placed in left target', 'Right frame can move LEFT/RIGHT within left panel', 'Barrier at cols 33-38 is continuous from rows 21-41', 'Frame cannot move on gray background areas']
- Open questions: ['How to get right frame past purple barrier to right target?', 'Is there a way to move the barrier or create a gap?', 'Can the left frame be moved out of its target to let right frame use it?']
- Plan: Try moving right frame RIGHT again from cols 27-29 to see if it can reach cols 30-32 and then through barrier. If blocked, consider moving left frame out of target and swapping positions.
- Strategy log: ['Attempted to move right frame directly RIGHT through barrier: blocked at cols 30-32', 'Attempted UP/DOWN on right frame: blocked by gray areas above and below corridor']
- Failed probes: ['UP on right frame at rows 30-32: no movement (step 24)', 'RIGHT on right frame at cols 30-32: no movement, barrier blocked (step 25)', 'DOWN on right frame at rows 30-32: no movement (step 26)', 'LEFT on right frame: moved back to cols 27-29 (step 27, not failed but retreated)']
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
The `world_model` tool argument is the reliable way to update the ledger. Assistant text is optional; if you write any, keep it short and prefix lines with `Action semantics:`, `Object taxonomy:`, `HUD map:`, `Win pattern:`, `Level log:`, `Cross-level notes:`, `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, or `Failed probes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 43 actions; a strong score needs about 28 or fewer. You are 1.5x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the image, I can see:
- The left frame (with white center, selected) is now on the right side, partially overlapping the right target (charcoal frame)
- The right frame (with black center, unselected) is still at rows 30-32, cols 30-32
Wait, looking more carefully at the image:
- The frame with white center (selected) is at the right side, near the target
- The frame with black center (unselected) is at cols 30-32
But from the segmentation data:
- N frame: rows=30-32, cols=30-32, children=[8] - this has child 8 (black center = unselected)
- N frame: rows=30-32, cols=42-44, children=[9] - this has child 9 (white center = selected)
So the selected frame is at cols 42-44, and the unselected is at cols 30-32.
The right target is at rows 26-30, cols 44-48. The selected frame is at rows 30-32, cols 42-44.
For the frame to be centered in the target:
- Target rows 26-30, so frame should be at rows 27-29
- Target cols 44-48, so frame should be at cols 45-47
Current: rows 30-32, cols 42-44
Need: UP (to rows 27-29), RIGHT (to cols 45-47)
But wait, I already did UP and RIGHT. Let me check - the frame moved from cols 42-44 to... Let me look at the image again.
Actually looking at the image, the selected frame (white center) appears to be right next to the target, not inside it. It seems like the UP move didn't work (blocked by gray area above) and the RIGHT move worked.
Let me check: the frame is at rows 30-32, cols 42-44. The target is at rows 26-30, cols 44-48. 
If I move UP, the frame would go to rows 27-29. But the target is at rows 26-30, so rows 27-29 would be inside the target vertically. And if I move RIGHT, cols would go to 45-47, which is inside the target horizontally (44-48).
The UP move might have failed because there's no white panel space above. Looking at the image, the selected frame appears to have shifted right but stayed at the same vertical position. The frame is now at rows 30-32, cols 45-47, which means the RIGHT move succeeded but UP didn't. I need to figure out how to get it vertically aligned with the target at rows 26-30. The frame is currently at rows 30-32, cols 45-47, but the target sits higher at rows 26-30. Moving UP should shift it to rows 27-29, which would center it vertically within the target. I'll attempt that move.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Selected frame at rows 30-32, cols 45-47 (after UP+RIGHT from 42-44)
# Target at rows 26-30, cols 44-48
# Need frame at rows 27-29, cols 45-47
# UP didn't work before (blocked), but let me check current position first
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
n_frames = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'N']
for n in n_frames:
    b = n['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    print(f"N frame: rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}, children={n['children']}")

# The selected frame (child 9) needs to go UP to rows 27-29
# Try UP again
action(['UP'])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>

Unselected frame at rows 30-32, cols 30-32. Right target at rows 26-30, cols 44-48. Left target at rows 32-36, cols 11-15.", "goal_model": "Move selected frame UP into right target, then move unselected frame to left target", "recent_findings": ["Selected frame reached right panel via barrier gap at rows 30-32"], "open_questions": ["Can frame move UP on right panel to reach target?"], "current_plan": "Try UP to move selected frame into right target", "strategy_log": ["Moved left frame through barrier gap to right panel"], "failed_probes": ["UP on right frame at rows 30-32 (left panel)", "DOWN on right frame", "RIGHT on right frame at cols 30-32"]}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
